ddls:
CREATE TABLE "templates" (
  "template_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each template */,
  "heuristic" TEXT /* Heuristic used to analyze the template (e.g., lexical_overlap) */,
  "template" TEXT /* Text template itself */,
  "subtemplate_id" INTEGER /* ID of the sub-template (if applicable) */,
  "label" TEXT /* Label assigned to the template (e.g., entailment, high-quality) */,
  "variables" TEXT /* List of variables used in the template */,
  "template_type" TEXT /* Type of the template (e.g., relative clause, simple sentence) */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of template creation */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of template update */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("template_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "variables" (
  "variable_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each variable */,
  "variable_name" TEXT /* Name of the variable (e.g., N1, Rels1) */,
  "variable_type" TEXT /* Type of the variable (e.g., noun, verb) */,
  "variable_description" TEXT /* Description of the variable */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("variable_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "variable_values" (
  "variable_value_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each variable value */,
  "template_id" INTEGER /* ID of the template associated with the variable value */,
  "variable_id" INTEGER /* ID of the variable associated with the value */,
  "variable_value" TEXT /* Value of the variable */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("variable_value_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_variable_values_template_id FOREIGN KEY ("template_id") REFERENCES templates ("template_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_variable_values_variable_id FOREIGN KEY ("variable_id") REFERENCES variables ("variable_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "examples" (
  "example_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each example */,
  "template_id" INTEGER /* ID of the template associated with the example */,
  "premise" TEXT /* Example premise */,
  "hypothesis" TEXT /* Example hypothesis */,
  "high_quality" TEXT /* High-quality analysis result */,
  "extreme_low_quality" TEXT /* Extreme low-quality analysis result */,
  "example_type" TEXT /* Type of the example (e.g., entailment, non-entailment) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("example_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_examples_template_id FOREIGN KEY ("template_id") REFERENCES templates ("template_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username chosen by the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., admin, contributor) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "submissions" (
  "submission_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each submission */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who made the submission */,
  "text" TEXT /* Text submitted by the user */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of the submission */,
  "submission_type" TEXT /* Type of the submission (e.g., text, template) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("submission_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_submissions_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "feedback" (
  "feedback_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each feedback entry */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who provided the feedback */,
  "submission_id" INTEGER /* ID of the submission associated with the feedback */,
  "feedback" TEXT /* Feedback provided by the user */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of the feedback */,
  "feedback_type" TEXT /* Type of the feedback (e.g., positive, negative) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("feedback_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_feedback_submission_id FOREIGN KEY ("submission_id") REFERENCES submissions ("submission_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_feedback_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "analysis_results" (
  "result_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each analysis result */,
  "submission_id" INTEGER /* ID of the submission associated with the result */,
  "result" TEXT /* Result of the text analysis */,
  "confidence" REAL /* Confidence score of the result */,
  "analysis_type" TEXT /* Type of the analysis (e.g., entailment, sentiment analysis) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("result_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_analysis_results_submission_id FOREIGN KEY ("submission_id") REFERENCES submissions ("submission_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "model_performance" (
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each performance metric */,
  "metric_name" TEXT /* Name of the performance metric (e.g., accuracy, F1 score) */,
  "value" REAL /* Value of the performance metric */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of the metric */,
  "model_version" TEXT /* Version of the model */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("metric_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "model_training_data" (
  "data_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each training data entry */,
  "template_id" INTEGER /* ID of the template associated with the training data */,
  "example_id" INTEGER /* ID of the example associated with the training data */,
  "submission_id" INTEGER /* ID of the submission associated with the training data */,
  "training_type" TEXT /* Type of the training data (e.g., labeled, unlabeled) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("data_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_model_training_data_submission_id FOREIGN KEY ("submission_id") REFERENCES submissions ("submission_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_model_training_data_template_id FOREIGN KEY ("template_id") REFERENCES templates ("template_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_model_training_data_example_id FOREIGN KEY ("example_id") REFERENCES examples ("example_id")
)
